ocr_text:
AT THE WHEEL
excerpt from A Macauley's s on plans for 1937

NEGUS MAY ATTEND LEAGUE ASSEMBLY; Mussolini Held Certain Not to Send Delegation if Emperor Is Accepted by Officials. COUNCIL TO MEET FRIDAY Non-Recognition of Ethiopia Is Italy's Price for Return to Geneva Discussions.
Emperor reptd planning to attend session with delegation

NEW SAFETY DRIVE IS URGED FOR CITY; Fowler Asks Insurance Firms to Use New York in Tests to Cut Auto Accidents. KNOX VIEWS ARE ASSAILED His Statement on Insurance Concerns Termed 'Asinine' -- Change in Ads Sought.
J A Peirce urges more appeal to women

FIRST LEGIONNAIRES REACH CLEVELAND; H.W. Colmery Is Reported Leading Field in Race for New Commander.
Says 250,000 will attend Amer Legion convention, Cleveland

HOLD SMITH STAND NO AID TO LANDON; Progressives Say Roosevelt Will Lose No Votes Which Are Not Already Gone.
D J Tobin charges coercion of indus workers against Roosevelt

To Join Royal Indemnity Board
To be elected a director of Royal Indemnity Co

LEGION AGAIN ASKS FOR DRIVE ON REDS; William Green Urges Action With Labor on Neutrality in War Looming in Europe.
S on veterans and labor uniting against any attempt to entangle U S abroad
Republican Program For the Opening Day

HAIL WOMEN'S PART IN PARTY PLATFORM; Republican Committee Women Express Satisfaction Over Bleakley Nomination. MRS. BACON ENTHUSIASTIC Vice Chairman Believes Ticket Will Aid Landon and Knox to Carry the State.
As vice chman of N Y S Repub convention, praises part played by women in adopting platform

LEHMAN OPENS CAMPAIGN WITH ATTACK ON BLEAKLEY; CALLS PROGRAM 'SUPINE'; STANDS ON OWN RECORD Courageous Leadership Executive's Duty, He Says in Acceptance. RIVAL'S PLAN HELD FUTILE Refusal to Appeal to People From Legislature Seen as Bar to Progressive Laws. NOTIFICATION BY FARLEY He Predicts Record Victory in State -- Other Candidates Speak at Ceremony Here. GOV. LEHMAN OPENS HIS CAMPAIGN HERE
Defends record in s at Natl Dem Club

INQUIRY IS ORDERED ON STEEL 'COERCION'; Senate Committee Will Go Into Charges That Pennsylvania Voters Are Intimidated. REPUBLICANS SEEK OFFSET Point to WPA Job Record and Assert Illiterate Foremen Are Employed on Projects.
Repubs urge Sen investigation of alleged coercion of Pa members by J L Lewis to vote for Roosevelt

Miss Fry's Question Mark Takes Hunter Prize at Rhinebeck Show; Morris Rides Gelding to Triumph in Redmond Trophy Event Before Gallery Including President's Mother -- 3 Blues Won by Marquetry -- Heinburg Injured in Fall From Mount.
Rhinebeck-Dutchess County Fair

In The Nation; Straw Polls Now Begin in Earnest
A Krock comments

Advertising News and Notes
Plans for regional midyr meetings

FARLEY HEARS CITY IS SAFE BY 835,000; Leaders Estimate Roosevelt May Even Better His 1932 Plurality of 871,000.
Holds conf of N Y C Dem leaders on Lehman and Roosevelt campaigns; stresses harmony with Tammany

10 UNIONS IN C.I.O. READ OUT OF A.F.L. WITH 'WAR' EDICT; Green Says Federation Will 'Carry on Aggressive Organizing in all Industries.' PREDICTS OPEN RIVALRY Lewis Leaders Deny Any Legal Severance and Demand Convention Decision. 10 UNIONS IN C.I.O. READ OUT OF A.F.L.
issues formal statement on withdrawal of CIO unions from AFL

WEST SEEN FOR ROOSEVELT; Mrs. Caspar Whitney Finds Swing for the New Deal.
Completes speaking tour of West; claims area for Roosevelt

SIFT TRADE ROUTES FOR ELIMINATIONS; Shipping Board Officials Gather Data to See if Any Should Lose Subsidy. PREPARING FOR NEW LAW Commission Yet to Be Named Will Carry Out Program to End Mail Contracts.
Shipping Bd Bur making study of trade routes to determine which are non-essential and for purpose of withholding subsidy to lines using those routes

IMMIGRATION CURB ON JEWS OPPOSED; 30 Senators and Representatives Express Concern Over Situation in Palestine. SEND MESSAGES TO HULL Call on Secretary of State to Urge Britain to Protect the Interests of Zionists.
Congressmen urge Brit govt to protect interests of Jews in Palestine

LANDON WILL CARRY FIGHT TO ROOSEVELT IN FARM COUNTRY; Governor Plans Return to Des Moines to Present Drought Program in Full. SPEAKS AT WICHITA TODAY Young Republicans of Nation Called to Topeka to Hear Address on Friday. LANDON TO CARRY FIGHT TO PRESIDENT
Plans for conf, Topeka

REPUBLICAN HOPES RISE IN FOUR VOTING TESTS; Dubious in Maine and Consoling in Wisconsin, They Mark Congress Gain In Massachusetts and Michigan NEW DEAL DOMINATION WANING
comment by A Krock

REPUBLICANS MOVE TO 'SAVE' KANSAS; Drive Starts as Leaders Cut Their Early Estimates of Big Landon Majority. STATE PRIDE IS A FACTOR Survey in Farming Area Shows New Deal and Drought Checks Aid Roosevelt Sentiment.
comment on sentiment; Repubs start drive

MORRIS IS ENDORSED BY CITY FUSION GROUP; Howe Re-elected as Chairman, Criticizes La Guardla, but Favors His Second Term.
Annual meeting; B Howe re-elected chmn; N Morris, Repub candidate for Aldermanic Pres, Seabury-Bearle transit unification plan and proposed new city charter endorsed

3 FRENCH PLANES OFF IN PARIS-SAIGON RACE; Michel Detroyat Is Expected to Win Round-Trip Contest Over 15,000 Miles.
3 planes take off on start of round trip Paris-Saigon race sponsored by French Air Ministry

NEUMANN CAPTURES 100-MILE AIR RACE; Kansas City Pilot Wins Shell Trophy as S.J. Wittman Makes Forced Landing. MISS BROWNING A VICTOR Forty Thousand See Wichita Girl Win Earhart Trophy at Los Angeles.
Mr and Mrs B O Howard hurt in Bendix race crash, Navajo Reservation

Whitney Again Will Lead American Riders Against Argentines at Westbury; SAME U.S. POLOISTS RIDE AGAIN TODAY Whitney's Offer to Withdraw From Second Contest With Argentina Refused. INVADERS FAVORED TO WIN Another Victory for Them at Westbury Will Send Trophy to South America.

Yanks 11-20 Favorites to Win World Series, but Giants Are Picked in Opener; SERIES SALE POINTS TO $1,000,000 GATE All Box Seats Disposed Of, With a Heavy Demand for Other Reserved Tickets. HOTELS FILLING RAPIDLY Hubbell Certain to Hurl First Game -- Yankee Selections Remain Unsettled.
$1,000,000 gate indicated by demand for tickets

MILK STRIKE START IN 10 DAYS HINTED; Piseck Brothers Predict That 'Holiday' Will Be 85 to 90% Effective In This State. FARMERS CHEER APPEAL 800 at Delhi Told It Is Their Turn to 'Squeeze' -- Experts Say City's Supply Is Assured. MILK STRIKE START IN TEN DAYS HINTED
Producers favor "holiday" in fight against classified price plan; shipments from Wis unlikely; City dealers expect no shortage

CHEERS GREET LINER AFTER RECORD TRIP; Throngs Watch the Queen Mary Enter Southampton in Triumph -- Owners Bar Contest.
cheered on arrival in Southampton; officials comment

FARLEY PREPARES DRIVE FOR STATE; First Campaign Conference With County Leaders to Be Held Here Saturday.
Speculation on Roosevelt's chances in N Y; labor vote likely to cut Landon plurality up-State
Business World

Oil-Burner Sales Increase
On oil-burner sales

$1,000,000,000 RISE IN DEFICIT FOR 1937 SEEN BY ROOSEVELT; RELIEF MAY COST $500,000,000; DEFICIT OF $2,096,996,300
Attacks revised budget statement issued by Roosevelt

New Madrid Drive Predicted
Reptd to have arrived in Valladolid to lead Spanish Rebel attack on Madrid
REAL ESTATE NOTES

SOMOZA WELCOMES ELECTION OBSERVERS; Sure People Will Make Him Nicaraguan President -- He Sees End of 'Bad Days.'
Somoza int; replies to Chamorro's statement; invites election observers

NEW DEAL STARTS NEGRO VOTE DRIVE; Stanley High Opens Movement to Swing Race in the North Behind President. MRS. ROOSEVELT SPEAKS 'Good Neighbor' Clubs Plan to Stop Their Members From 'Voting for Lincoln.'
Negro com to support Roosevelt formed, at N Y C meeting
